title: Strychnodaphne suaveolens
taxonomy: Strychnodaphne suaveolens is classified within the family Lauraceae. This classification places it in the order Laurales, which is a group of flowering plants. The Lauraceae family is a large and diverse group of evergreen trees and shrubs. The precise classification of Strychnodaphne suaveolens is still subject to scientific study and verification.

side_effects: Strychnodaphne suaveolens, a plant native to tropical Africa, contains toxic compounds that can cause severe side effects if ingested. intro: Strychnodaphne suaveolens is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Strychnodaphne suaveolens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
